commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6b3c8a1a0eb76b6c90d71016a52f640ffa37fddc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a hard-coded compaction chunk failure counter and circuit-breaker error in `summarizeChunks()`, plus a new Vitest test file for unavailable summarizer cases.

PR surface: Source +29, Tests +162. Total +191 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current `summarizeInStages()` calls `summarizeWithFallback()` for each split and then for the merge, while `retryAsync()` gives each call three attempts; the PR blocker is also source-visible because `summarizeChunks()` still throws after the first failed chunk.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the branch needs a real run-level compaction fix and contributor-supplied behavior proof before any repair or merge path is useful.

target.title: fix(compaction): add circuit breaker to stop token burn when summarizer unavailable
target.body:
## Summary

When the summarizer model is persistently unavailable (rate-limited, overloaded,
misconfigured), `summarizeChunks()` retries each chunk 3 times before failing.
With N chunks, this burns N × 3 × chunkTokens before finally throwing.

This adds a circuit breaker that tracks consecutive chunk failures:
- After 2 consecutive chunk failures, stop the chunk loop immediately
- Return the best available partial summary or previous summary
- Reset the counter on successful chunks to handle intermittent failures

## What changed

- Added `consecutiveChunkFailures` counter in `summarizeChunks()`
- Circuit breaker triggers at `COMPACTION_CIRCUIT_BREAKER_THRESHOLD` (2)
- On trigger, throw `CompactionCircuitBreakerError` with `partialSummary`
  attached so `summarizeWithFallback()` can use it
- Counter resets to 0 on successful chunk (intermittent failures are fine)
- 4 new tests in `compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`

## Before / After

**Before**: summarizer unavailable, 5 chunks → 5 × 3 = 15 API calls, 0 progress
**After**: summarizer unavailable → chunk 1 fails 3 retries → chunk 2 fails 3 retries → circuit breaker opens → fallback to previous/partial summary

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Compaction summarizer model unavailable (rate-limited, overloaded, misconfigured) → every chunk retries 3 times → N chunks × 3 retries burns tokens with no progress. 5-chunk compaction wastes ~60K tokens.

**Real environment tested**: Linux Node 22.22.0, standalone proof script simulating compaction chunk loop with circuit breaker behavior from this patch.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```sh
node src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.proof.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
=== WITHOUT circuit breaker (5 chunks, all fail) ===
summarizeChunks iterates over 5 chunks, each retries 3 times:
  [chunk-1] attempt 1/3 → FAIL
  [chunk-1] attempt 2/3 → FAIL
  [chunk-1] attempt 3/3 → FAIL
  [chunk-2] attempt 1/3 → FAIL
  ...
  [chunk-5] attempt 3/3 → FAIL
  Result: 15 API calls, all chunks failed, summary NOT generated
  Token waste: 5 chunks × 3 retries × 4K tokens = ~60K tokens burned

=== WITH circuit breaker (threshold=2 consecutive failures) ===
summarizeChunks with circuit breaker:
  [chunk-1] attempt 1/3 → FAIL
  [chunk-1] attempt 2/3 → FAIL
  [chunk-1] attempt 3/3 → FAIL
  consecutive failures: 1/2

  [chunk-2] attempt 1/3 → FAIL
  [chunk-2] attempt 2/3 → FAIL
  [chunk-2] attempt 3/3 → FAIL
  consecutive failures: 2/2

  ⚡ CIRCUIT BREAKER TRIGGERED at chunk "chunk-2"
  stopping chunk loop, returning partial/previous summary
  Result: ~6 API calls before circuit breaker opened
  Token saved: ~36K tokens (remaining 3 chunks skipped)

  ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ Before: 5 chunks × 3 retries × 4K    │
  │         = ~60K tokens wasted         │
  ├──────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ After:  2 chunks × 3 retries × 4K    │
  │         = ~24K tokens + fallback     │
  │         = ~36K tokens saved          │
  └──────────────────────────────────────┘

=== PASS: circuit breaker stops token burn ===
```

**Observed result after fix**: Without the circuit breaker, all 5 chunks fail 3 times each (15 API calls, 60K+ tokens burned). With the circuit breaker, after 2 consecutive chunk failures (6 API calls), the loop stops and returns a partial/previous summary — saving ~36K tokens.

**What was not tested**: Live compaction with a real summarizer model flipping between available/unavailable. The circuit breaker logic is deterministic (consecutive failure counter + threshold), and the unit tests cover the three code paths (all fail, partial fail, success-after-failure).

## Verification

```
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 npx vitest run src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts
```

Fixes #58838
target.author: tanshanshan
